title: FCC approves first Reflect Orbital satellite
summary: The FCC has approved the first Reflect Orbital satellite, which aims to reflect sunlight into nighttime regions. The project has faced criticism from astronomers and environmentalists.

title: Earth observation satellites pass telecom in European space industry sales
summary: European space industry sales rebounded in 2025 after a contraction in 2024, driven by a surge in Earth observation satellites surpassing telecom sales. The growth was highlighted in Eurospace's latest Facts and Figures report.

title: China unveils members of state-backed commercial space consortium
summary: China's government published a membership list for a state-backed commercial space consortium, revealing key players in the sector. This move highlights the country's strategy to foster commercial space development through state support.

title: The space industry is weighing ambitious hiring against heritage
summary: The space industry is balancing ambitious hiring practices with the need to preserve its heritage and expertise. The challenge lies in maintaining traditional 'rocket science' skills while adapting to modern demands.

title: Startup testing nuclear battery technology in orbit
summary: City Labs is testing a tritium-powered nuclear battery in orbit to validate its use for satellites and lunar systems. This technology could provide power where solar energy is insufficient.

title: Space capitalism needs more than a bull market
summary: The article discusses the thriving state of commercial space, highlighting recent regulatory activity in Washington, including executive orders and directives. It argues that space capitalism requires more than just a bull market to succeed.

title: ElevationSpace advances work on commercial reentry vehicle
summary: ElevationSpace, a Japanese startup, is developing commercial reentry vehicles and securing customers while monitoring SpaceX's market entry. The company is also preparing for its first mission.

title: China becomes second country to recover orbital booster with Long March 10B
summary: China successfully launched and recovered the first stage of its Long March 10B rocket, marking a significant milestone in the country's reusable rocket development. This achievement makes China the second country to recover an orbital booster.

title: Interior Department requests information on offshore launch options
summary: The U.S. Department of the Interior is soliciting information on offshore orbital launch concepts to alleviate congestion at existing spaceports. This initiative is part of a broader effort to expand launch capabilities.

title: SDA TAP Lab Evolves Into BMC3I TAP Lab And In Partnership With Catalyst Campus Launches A New Multi-Phased Program To Accelerate Mission-Focused Technology Development
summary: The SDA TAP Lab has rebranded to the BMC3I TAP Lab and launched a new multi-phased program with Catalyst Campus to accelerate mission-focused technology development through collaboration between commercial innovators and government.
company: Catalyst Campus for Technology and Innovation (CCTI)

title: White House appoints Harvard astronomer Avi Loeb to lead new UFO study group
summary: The White House has appointed Harvard astronomer Avi Loeb to lead a new group focused on studying unidentified anomalous phenomena (UFOs). This initiative aims to investigate unexplained aerial phenomena with scientific rigor.

title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launches for 35th time, hauls Starlink satellites to orbit (video)
summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket completed its 35th launch, deploying 29 Starlink satellites into low Earth orbit. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's reusable rocket program.

title: A ravenous black hole in our backyard could be our window into the ancient universe
summary: A nearby galaxy's feeding black hole mimics the behavior of ancient cosmic titans from just after the Big Bang. This discovery could provide insights into the early universe.

title: Launching from 2 continents: Germany's Isar Aerospace leases Canadian pad for $150 million
summary: German company Isar Aerospace has signed a $150 million agreement with Canada's Maritime Launch Services to lease a launch pad in Nova Scotia, expanding its operations to two continents. This move marks Isar Aerospace's first overseas expansion.

title: SpaceX ignites all 33 powerful engines on Starship booster ahead of Flight 13 launch (video)
summary: SpaceX successfully test-fired all 33 engines on the next Super Heavy booster for Starship ahead of Flight 13. The launch could occur as soon as next week.

title: SpaceX launch creates colorful 'jellyfish' in the night sky | Space photo of the day for July 10, 2026
summary: A SpaceX launch created a visually striking 'jellyfish' effect in the night sky, captured in a space photo. The phenomenon was later confirmed to be unrelated to extraterrestrial activity.

title: Supreme Court ruling on mail-in ballots ensures astronauts can vote from space  — or anywhere else
summary: A U.S. Supreme Court ruling protecting mail-in ballots may allow astronauts to vote from space or anywhere else. The decision could extend voting rights to astronauts training internationally.

title: Astronomers may have heard the 1st 'whispers' of ghost particles created by supernova explosions
summary: Astronomers may have detected the first signals of neutrinos, often called 'ghost particles,' which are remnants of supernova explosions from billions of years ago. These elusive particles could provide insights into the deaths of stars and the history of the universe.

title: Early Career Faculty (ECF) 2025 Awards
summary: NASA's Early Career Faculty (ECF) 2025 Awards will fund advanced diagnostics for high-enthalpy test facilities simulating spacecraft atmospheric entry and research on autonomous spacecraft using machine learning for onboard guidance, navigation, and control.

title: NASA Volunteers Help Zooniverse Reach 1 Billion Classifications
summary: NASA volunteers have helped Zooniverse, a NASA-funded research platform, reach 1 billion classifications through crowd-sourced citizen science. This milestone highlights the global impact of public participation in space and astronomy research.

title: NASA Photographer Captures Images from F-18 Over Washington
summary: NASA photographers captured unique aerial images of the agency's missions from an F-18 over Washington. These photos provide rare behind-the-scenes views of NASA's research and operations.

title: Waxing Gibbous Moon
summary: The image shows the waxing gibbous moon from the International Space Station, located 264 miles above the Indian Ocean southeast of Madagascar on June 26, 2026. This lunar phase occurs before the full moon and is characterized by the moon's increased brightness.

title: Where Venezuela’s Earthquakes Shifted the Ground
summary: Radar data from the NISAR satellite revealed significant ground displacement in La Guaira and nearby areas due to the June 2026 earthquakes in Venezuela. The findings highlight the satellite's capability to monitor seismic activity and its impacts.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Anil Menon Launch to Space Station
summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon is set to launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on July 14, joining Expedition 74 to advance scientific research. The mission will be accompanied by cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina.

title: NASA Space Telescope Maps Magnetic Fields of ‘Lighthouse’ Pulsar
summary: NASA's IXPE has directly measured the magnetic fields of PSR J1101−6101, a pulsar in the Lighthouse Nebula, offering new insights into extreme cosmic objects.

title: Principal Investigator and Quality Assessment Reports Evaluate Umbra Synthetic Aperture Radar Data
summary: Principal Investigator and Quality Assessment Reports evaluate Umbra's Synthetic Aperture Radar data, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research and applications.

title: Curiosity Sees Martian Sulfur Up Close
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover discovered fragments of sulfur crystals on the Red Planet after accidentally crushing a rock on May 30, 2024. This marks the first time such crystals have been observed on Mars.

title: NASA Scientists Take to Air and Space to Study Arctic Sea Ice
summary: NASA engineers are testing a spacecraft sensor to measure Arctic sea ice loss, with field research conducted in the Canadian wilderness ahead of its planned launch next year.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: SpaceX completed a record-duration static fire test of Booster 20 using 33 engines. This marks a significant milestone in the development of the Starship program.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays. The company reported significant gains in Electron launches during this period.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant progress with new launch pads being developed for Stoke Space and Relativity Space, contributing to the region's revitalization. Multiple launch providers are advancing their facilities at Cape Canaveral.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station aims to serve as a private orbital destination ahead of larger Vast space station plans.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress to meet this timeline.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative in June 2026. This marks a significant step toward establishing a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent New Glenn anomaly that caused significant damage. The company aims to restore operations at the facility promptly.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Several orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to save a NASA space telescope, marking its first mission since 2021.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 Council Meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities. The events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed to enhance space operations. This innovation is expected to improve efficiency and capabilities in satellite servicing and other space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway, supporting scientific research in space. The programme facilitates participation of research institutes in European space science missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space. Their work supports the success of ESA's missions by ensuring spacecraft components are space-proofed.

title: Pulse Space Lands $40M Space Force Contract to Develop Laser Tech
summary: Pulse Space, a Washington-based startup, secured a $40 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop laser technology for power beaming between satellites. The funding will advance their mission to enable in-orbit energy transfer.

title: US Government Studying Offshore Launch Opportunities
summary: The U.S. government is exploring offshore launch opportunities by studying the potential for building or repurposing infrastructure on the Outer Continental Shelf for space launches and reentries. The Bureau of Ocean Energy Management (BOEM) issued a Request for Information (RFI) to gather relevant data.

title: NATO Allies Plan to Stitch Together Sovereign Space Fleets
summary: Eight NATO allies announced plans to integrate their national defense satellites into a unified network. This initiative aims to enhance collective space-based defense capabilities among member states.

title: Blue Origin Seeks $10B at $130B Valuation, Reporting Shows
summary: Blue Origin is seeking $10 billion in funding at a $130 billion valuation, marking a shift away from Jeff Bezos' personal funding. This signals a major financial move as the company transitions from a billion-dollar pet project.

title: Auxilium Demos Tech To Enable Sustainable Bioprinting in Orbit
summary: Auxilium Biotechnologies successfully created multiple types of organ tissues aboard the ISS, demonstrating scalable bioprinting technology for future orbital applications.

title: ispace Launches Lunar Integrator Service With Starship
summary: ispace has secured 500 kg of payload capacity on SpaceX's Starship for lunar missions, targeting a launch no earlier than 2030. This initiative marks ispace's expansion into a lunar integrator service.

title: Venus Aerospace Raises $91M Series B to Scale RDRE Production
summary: Venus Aerospace has raised $91 million in a Series B funding round to scale production of its Rotating Detonation Rocket Engine (RDRE) technology. The investment highlights growing industry interest in the more efficient rocket propulsion method.

title: FCC Poised To Advance Sat Licensing Reform
summary: The FCC is set to vote on the Space Modernization Order on July 22, aiming to reform satellite licensing. FCC Chair Brendan Carr announced the upcoming decision.

title: Transporter-17 Sends the First Commercial Nuclear Sat to Orbit
summary: SpaceX's Transporter-17 rideshare mission launched 81 payloads to LEO, including the first commercial nuclear satellite. The liftoff occurred at 3:12am from Vandenberg Space Force Base.

title: NASA Releases CLD Draft RFP
summary: NASA is advancing its plan to replace the ISS with stand-alone commercial habitats in LEO after industry feedback validated human habitation concepts. The agency has released a draft Request for Proposal (RFP) for the Commercial Low Earth Orbit (CLD) program.

title: FCC Approves Reflect Orbital Demo Satellite to Test Redirecting Sunlight
summary: The FCC approved Reflect Orbital's demonstration satellite to test redirecting sunlight from orbit back to Earth. This marks a step in solar reflector technology development.

title: Space42 and Skylo Test D2D Test on Thuraya-4
summary: Space42 and Skylo successfully tested direct-to-device messaging (SMS and SOS) on the Thuraya-4 satellite. The partners announced the achievement on July 9 and plan to expand the service.

title: Ovzon’s GEO Connectivity and Terminals Complete Arctic Tests
summary: Ovzon has successfully completed Arctic tests for its GEO connectivity and terminals, demonstrating technology performance in extreme environments. The company conducted three High North expeditions to validate its systems.

title: Simera Sense to Supply Imagers for Canary Islands Satellite Constellation
summary: Simera Sense will supply multispectral imagers for the Canary Islands satellite constellation project. Telespazio Ibérica has selected Simera Sense for this role.

title: MDA Space Vertically Integrates in EO Analytics With Deal to Acquire Majority of CLS
summary: MDA Space is acquiring a majority stake in French Earth observation analytics company CLS to vertically integrate in Earth Observation (EO) analytics. This move strengthens MDA Space's position in the EO data processing and insight generation sector.

title: SpaceX Files for 100,000-Satellite Gen-3 Constellation
summary: SpaceX has submitted a filing with the FCC to launch a third-generation satellite constellation consisting of 100,000 satellites for high-speed internet services. The Gen3 constellation aims to provide 'multi-gigabit' connectivity using W- and D-band frequencies.

title: Telesat to Provide Mil-Ka Arctic Connectivity for Canada’s ESCP-P Program
summary: Telesat has secured a deal with the Canadian government to supply Military Ka-band satellite capacity for Canada's Enhanced Satellite Communications Project – Polar (ESCP-P) program. The agreement aims to enhance Arctic connectivity for military and government use.

title: Rivada Signs Connectivity Partner in Nepal
summary: Rivada Space Networks has partnered with a telecoms company in Nepal to provide connectivity services. The deal involves Mercantile Space, a communications technology firm in the country with a population of around 30 million.

title: Impulse Space and Relativity Space Join the Field for Space Force NSSL Phase 3, Lane 1
summary: Impulse Space and Relativity Space have joined the competition for the U.S. Space Force's National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Phase 3, Lane 1 contracts. The field of eligible companies for these awards has now expanded to seven.

title: Beyond Gravity Signs Gamechanging Contract with Ariane Group
summary: Beyond Gravity has signed a major contract with Ariane Group, solidifying its role in the European launch supply chain. The agreement is one of the largest in Beyond Gravity's history.
